message:
feat(transport,ci): fix framed transport partial read tracking and add browser/native compatibility matrix

Transport: asx_framed_transport_read was not tracking partial progress —
when the inner transport returned fewer bytes than the pending frame length,
the function returned ASX_E_PENDING but on the next call started reading the
full frame again from scratch, causing duplicate reads. Now decrements
pending_frame_len by the number of bytes actually received and resets the
frame state only when all bytes have arrived.

CI: Extends the minimal-browser focused test lane (BROWSER_MINIMAL_FOCUSED_
TEST_BIN) with 11 native-hidden test targets that verify browser-excluded
surfaces compile-hide correctly under ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL.

Docs: Adds section 8.2 "Explicit Browser/Native Compatibility Matrix" to
PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md — a single table declaring which
surface/selection combinations are supported, denied, or unimplemented across
native, browser-extended, and browser-minimal profiles. This replaces the
previous implicit-absence approach with an explicit contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -267,7 +267,7 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 
 | Upstream contract | Current ANSI C equivalent | State | Evidence / gap |
 |---|---|---|---|
-| `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` plus `test-browser-minimal-focused` CI/test lanes in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections, browser builds now expose two compile-time browser subprofiles via `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 2` with default-extended semantics, and the new minimal-browser hidden-contract lane exercises `test_browser_boundary`, `encoding`, `decoding`, `lab`, `telemetry`, `regression_localize`, `replay`, and `tracing_compat`; remaining debt is deeper upstream feature granularity beyond the new minimal/extended split |
+| `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` plus `test-browser-minimal-focused` CI/test lanes in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections, browser builds now expose two compile-time browser subprofiles via `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 2` with default-extended semantics, and the minimal-browser hidden-contract lane now continuously proves both trace/io subprofile hiding plus the browser-hidden native families via `test_browser_boundary`, `test_blocking`, `test_io_driver`, `test_cli`, `test_fs`, `test_process`, `test_signal`, `test_server`, `test_grpc`, `test_messaging`, `test_tls`, `test_db`, `encoding`, `decoding`, `lab`, `telemetry`, `regression_localize`, `replay`, and `tracing_compat`; remaining debt is deeper upstream feature granularity beyond the new minimal/extended split |
 | `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `cli`, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, `include/asx/app/app.h` no longer drags `fs.h`/`process.h`/`signal.h` outside its native-surface guard, and `include/asx/fs/fs.h`, `include/asx/process/process.h`, `include/asx/signal/signal.h`, `include/asx/app/app.h`, `include/asx/cli/cli.h`, `include/asx/runtime/io_driver.h`, `include/asx/runtime/blocking.h`, `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, `include/asx/net/tls.h`, and `include/asx/net/db.h` now self-gate their native families directly. `tests/unit/runtime/test_rt.c` now also honors those compile-time-hidden blocking and IO-driver surfaces instead of assuming the browser umbrella still exposes native-only declarations; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
 | `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Minimal-browser now gates the adapter-based public I/O family at compile time | `partial` | `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO == 0` under `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL`, `include/asx/asx.h` omits `bytes/io_adapter`, `encoding`, and `decoding` from the umbrella in that subprofile, the direct `include/asx/bytes/io_adapter.h`, `include/asx/encoding/encoding.h`, and `include/asx/decoding/decoding.h` headers now self-gate there, and `encoding.h` / `decoding.h` no longer drag `io_adapter.h` outside that subprofile guard; focused unit suites plus `test_browser_boundary` prove the split, and remaining debt is broader upstream browser feature granularity beyond this adapter-based I/O family |
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Browser minimal-vs-extended subprofiles now gate the public trace family | `partial` | `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE == 0` under `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL`, `include/asx/asx.h` omits `trace`/`telemetry`/`replay`/`lab`/`regression_localize`/`tracing_compat` from the umbrella in minimal browser builds, the direct headers now self-gate those families unless internal implementation files opt in via `ASX_INTERNAL_TRACE_FAMILY_ACCESS`, and `telemetry.h` / `replay.h` / `regression_localize.h` / `tracing_compat.h` no longer drag `trace.h` or `lab.h` outside those guards; remaining debt is broader upstream feature parity beyond this trace-family split |
@@ -280,14 +280,33 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 
 ### 8.1 Missing Fail-Closed Checks Before Parity Claims
 
-The current C tree still needs these explicit fail-closed controls before it
+The current C tree still needs this explicit fail-closed control before it
 can honestly claim equivalence with the upstream feature/platform matrix:
 
 1. Explicit compile-time removal of browser-incompatible APIs from the public
    browser umbrella/header surface rather than relying on capability macros plus
    runtime fail-closed boundary and browser-focused test lanes.
-2. A single compatibility matrix that states which combinations are supported,
-   denied, or currently unimplemented rather than leaving absence implicit.
+
+### 8.2 Explicit Browser/Native Compatibility Matrix
+
+This table is the single support-status contract for the currently shipped
+browser/native surface combinations. `supported` means the public surface is
+intentionally available in that mode, `denied` means the public contract
+compile-hides or fail-closes it in that mode, and `unimplemented` means the
+selection is modeled explicitly but still returns a stable unsupported status.
+
+| Surface / selection | Native profiles (`CORE`/`POSIX`/etc.) | Browser extended | Browser minimal | Contract status | Enforcement |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| Core/browser-safe umbrella families (`actor`, `sync`, `bytes` buf/codec, `stream`, `security`, `plan`, `cx`, `link`, `net`, `http`, `web`, `websocket`, `quic`, `distributed`, `pipe`, `service`, `transport`, `remote`, `spork`, `console`, `evidence`, `monitor`, `record`, `migration`, `raptorq`) | supported | supported | supported | supported | Public headers stay visible and focused browser lanes run them continuously |
+| Native host/runtime families (`fs`, `process`, `signal`, `cli`, native `app`, `blocking`, `io_driver`) | supported | denied | denied | denied on browser | `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES`, `ASX_HAS_BLOCKING_SURFACE`, and `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER` gate the umbrella/direct headers and browser-focused lanes prove the hidden contract |
+| Native network/service families (`server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`) | supported | denied | denied | denied on browser | `ASX_HAS_*_SURFACE` macros gate the umbrella/direct headers and status-returning entry points fail closed |
+| Browser adapter-I/O families (`io_adapter`, `encoding`, `decoding`) | supported | supported | denied | minimal-browser denied | `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO` gates umbrella/direct headers and `test-browser-minimal-focused` proves the hidden contract |
+| Browser trace families (`trace`, `telemetry`, `replay`, `lab`, `regression_localize`, `tracing_compat`) | supported | supported | denied | minimal-browser denied | `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE` gates umbrella/direct headers and internal files opt in with `ASX_INTERNAL_TRACE_FAMILY_ACCESS` |
+| Runtime IO backend = `ghost` | supported | supported | supported | supported | Builder/runtime config accepts it and `rt` bootstraps successfully |
+| Runtime IO backend = `io-uring` | unimplemented | denied | denied | explicit unsupported selection | `ASX_HAS_IO_URING == 0`; selecting `ASX_IO_BACKEND_IO_URING` returns `ASX_E_PERMISSION_DENIED` |
+| TLS root source = `native` / `webpki` | unimplemented | denied | denied | explicit unsupported selection | `ASX_HAS_TLS_NATIVE_ROOTS == 0`, `ASX_HAS_TLS_WEBPKI_ROOTS == 0`; `asx_tls_config_set_root_source()` returns `ASX_E_PERMISSION_DENIED` |
+| DB backend = `sqlite` / `postgres` / `mysql` | unimplemented | denied | denied | explicit unsupported selection | `ASX_HAS_DB_*_BACKEND == 0`; `asx_db_pool_set_backend()` returns `ASX_E_PERMISSION_DENIED` |
+| Messaging backend = `kafka` | unimplemented | denied | denied | explicit unsupported selection | `ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_KAFKA_BACKEND == 0`; `asx_msg_broker_set_backend()` returns `ASX_E_PERMISSION_DENIED` |
